description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: Symptom: A link cannot be established. Scenario: This issue occurs when connecting a 100G-FR1 transceiver to a S0E91 on ports 49-52 and connecting the other end to a 4x100G-FR1 transceiver. Workaround: Toggle the 6300 port, change out the transceiver, or reboot.
scenario: This issue occurs when connecting a 100G-FR1 transceiver to a S0E91 on ports 49-52 and connecting the other end to a 4x100G-FR1 transceiver.
workaround: Toggle the 6300 port, change out the transceiver, or reboot.

description: Symptom: The switch crashes with an "Error during L1 configuration" message. Scenario: This issue may occur when shutting down a port. Workaround: The switch automatically reboots. Upgrade the switch software to a build with the SDK patch to avoid this issue.

workaround: The switch automatically reboots. Upgrade the switch software to a build with the SDK patch to avoid this issue.

description: Symptom: Classifier or PBR Policy is not enforced for traffic coming via ISL link. Scenario: In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting the classifier policy enforced. Workaround: To prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL, use active gateway instead of VRRP. This provides better active-active redundancy and packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to active gateway.
